default.name: $0-50/month
default.context: Typical for Light track personal projects. Free tiers of Vercel, Supabase, Railway cover most MVPs.

default.name: $50-500/month
default.context: Standard track with moderate traffic. Covers paid tiers of hosting, database, and monitoring services.

default.name: $500+/month
default.context: Full track or enterprise internal tools with SLA requirements. Covers dedicated infrastructure and support contracts.
default.when: Full track, enterprise, or high-availability requirements

default.name: 4-6 weeks
default.context: Realistic for a 3-5 feature MVP with one person working 10-15 hours/week. Includes planning phases.

default.name: 8-12 weeks
default.context: Standard timeline for 5-10 features including security validation and user testing.

default.name: 12-20 weeks
default.context: Full track with enterprise governance, pen testing, and formal approval cycles.

default.name: WCAG AA, Lighthouse 90+
default.context: Industry standard. Covers most accessibility requirements. Required by many organizations.

default.name: WCAG AAA
default.context: Highest accessibility standard. Significantly more effort. Required for government or healthcare.

default.name: Basic (keyboard navigation + screen reader)
default.context: Minimum viable accessibility. Not recommended for external-facing apps.
default.when: Internal tools with known user base

default.name: Best effort (99%)
default.context: ~7 hours downtime/month. Appropriate for internal tools and personal projects. No pager required.

default.name: Standard (99.9%)
default.context: ~43 minutes downtime/month. Standard for SaaS products. Requires monitoring and alerting.

default.name: High availability (99.99%+)
default.context: ~4 minutes downtime/month. The framework explicitly says it is not designed for this. Consider a dedicated platform team.
default.when: Not recommended — see framework Known Limitations

default.name: Public
default.context: No access controls needed. Safe to cache, log, and display freely.

default.name: Internal
default.context: Requires authentication to access. Standard access controls.

default.name: Confidential
default.context: Restricted access, audit logging, encryption at rest. Need-to-know basis.
default.when: Financial data, trade secrets, strategic plans

default.name: PII (Personally Identifiable Information)
default.context: Subject to privacy laws (GDPR, CCPA, etc.). Requires consent, retention policies, right to deletion.
default.when: Names, emails, addresses, phone numbers, government IDs

default.name: Daily automated backups with 30-day retention
default.context: Standard for most applications. Most managed databases (Supabase, PlanetScale, Railway) include this.

default.name: Point-in-time recovery
default.context: Restore to any second within retention window. Available on Supabase Pro and most managed Postgres providers.

default.name: Manual exports only
default.context: No automated backup. You export when you remember. Only appropriate for non-critical personal tools.
default.when: Personal tools with easily recreated data

default.name: github
default.context: Most common, best tooling (gh CLI), widely supported integrations

default.name: gitlab
default.context: Popular for self-hosted; glab CLI supported

default.name: bitbucket
default.context: Bitbucket Cloud via curl + App Password

default.name: other
default.context: Self-hosted Gitea, Codeberg, etc. — URL-paste + manual branch-protection attestation

default.name: private
default.context: Recommended for all projects. Org mode forces private.

default.name: public
default.context: Open-source projects only
